title: Review the current x-auto hardening state and recommend the minimum Notion DB/UI changes needed so schedule_mode/operator override are explicit to operators. Focus on columns/views only. Return a concise recommendation with rationale; do not edit anything.
summary_text: Current hardening state (as implemented) is already strict: 1. Off-slot approved posts fail unless `schedule_mode=operator_override` in health/scheduler checks ([x-auto-health.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator-public/scripts/eval/x-auto-health.py#L244), [scheduler.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scheduler.py#L362)). 2. Off-slot operator override is expected to include a reason (`schedule_override_reason`) in bundle prep/tests ([prepare_post_bundle.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/scripts/prepare_post_bundle.py#L87), [test_prepare_post_bundle.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/tests/test_prepare_post_bundle.py#L244)). 3. Notion sync already supports `Schedule Mode` and `Schedule Override Reason` properties ([notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L303), [notion_sync.py](/Users/masayuki/Dev/x-auto/notion_sync.py#L425)). 4. Live queue right now: 24 approved pending, all `fixed_slots`, 0 overrides (health check passed with today at `21:00`). Minimum Notion DB/UI changes (columns/views only): 1. Ensure `Schedule Mode` (Select) is present and visible in operator views. 2. Ensure `Schedule Override Reason` (Rich text) is present and visible next to it. 3. Add one formula column `Schedule Policy` to classify rows: `OK_FIXED`, `OK_OVERRIDE`, `MISSING_SCHEDULE`, `OFFSLOT_NEEDS_OVERRIDE`, `OVERRIDE_REASON_MISSING`. 4. Add/adjust two views: - `Scheduler Ready`: `Status=approved` and `Schedule Policy` in `OK_*`. - `Override Review`: `Status=approved` and `Schedule Policy` in non-OK states. Rationale: this is the smallest UI/schema change that makes operator intent explicit before runtime gates fire, aligns with existing hardening logic, and avoids silent off-slot failures without changing runtime behavior.
